probable_causes: Bearing wear or damage causing increased clearance
Rotor imbalance due to blade erosion or deposit buildup
Misalignment of coupling between turbine and generator
Oil film instability (oil whirl/oil whip) in journal bearings
diagnostic_procedures: Analyze vibration spectrum for dominant frequencies (1X, 2X, sub-synchronous)
Check bearing metal temperature trends and oil drain temperature
Inspect bearing clearances using lead wire or feeler gauge method
Verify coupling alignment readings (angular and offset)
Review rotor eccentricity readings during startup/shutdown
corrective_actions: Replace damaged bearings — match OEM specifications for clearance
Perform in-situ or shop rotor balancing
Realign coupling as per BHEL alignment procedure
Adjust bearing oil supply pressure and temperature
preventive_actions: Implement online vibration monitoring with trip/alarm settings
Schedule bearing inspections every major overhaul (typically 4-6 years)
Track vibration trend data for early anomaly detection
Maintain turbine oil quality — filtration, moisture removal, acidity check
safety_precautions: Ensure lockout/tagout before any bearing inspection
Verify oil system depressurized before opening bearing pedestals
Use proper lifting equipment for heavy bearing components

probable_causes: Solid particle erosion from boiler tube oxide scale
Wet steam erosion in LP turbine last-stage blades
Foreign object damage (FOD) from debris in steam path
Stress corrosion cracking at blade root attachments
diagnostic_procedures: Perform boroscopic inspection of blade rows without opening casing
Check condenser vacuum and LP exhaust conditions
Review steam purity data — silica, sodium, conductivity
Analyze blade surface for pitting, thinning, or crack indications
corrective_actions: Replace eroded or damaged blades with new set
Install erosion-resistant coatings (stellite shields) on LP last-stage blades
Clean steam path of debris and oxide scale
Repair blade root serrations if within limits
preventive_actions: Maintain strict boiler water and steam chemistry control
Perform chemical cleaning of boiler before oxide scale reaches critical levels
Install steam path strainers where feasible
Schedule boroscopic inspections during annual outages
safety_precautions: Never enter turbine casing without proper confined space and fall protection
Ensure casing is cooled to safe temperature before opening

probable_causes: Gland seal ring wear or damage
Excessive rotor runout causing seal rubbing
diagnostic_procedures: Check gland exhaust fan suction pressure
Inspect gland seal rings for wear during shutdown
Verify gland steam supply pressure and temperature
Monitor condenser vacuum stability for signs of air ingress
corrective_actions: Correct rotor runout if exceeding limits
Adjust gland steam supply control valve settings
Inspect and correct casing alignment if distorted
preventive_actions: Monitor gland condenser level and vacuum trends regularly
Include gland seal inspection in every major overhaul scope
Ensure proper warming procedure to minimize casing thermal distortion
safety_precautions: Be aware of hot steam leakage hazard near gland areas during operation
Use proper PPE when inspecting gland seal area

probable_causes: Water ingress from gland seal leakage or cooler tube leak
Varnish and sludge formation from oil degradation
Particle contamination from bearing wear or external sources
Oil cooler tube failure mixing cooling water with oil
diagnostic_procedures: Test oil for moisture content (Karl Fischer), acidity (TAN), particle count
Inspect oil cooler tubes for leakage using pressure test
Check oil reservoir for water accumulation at bottom
Review oil filter differential pressure trending
corrective_actions: Replace contaminated oil charge after thorough system flushing
Repair or plug leaking oil cooler tubes
Install or upgrade oil purification system (centrifuge, vacuum dehydrator)
Replace oil filters and clean strainers
preventive_actions: Implement regular oil sampling and analysis program (monthly)
Maintain oil temperature within recommended range to reduce degradation
Run oil purification system continuously during operation
Schedule oil cooler tube inspection during planned outages
safety_precautions: Oil is flammable — ensure fire protection systems are available
Avoid hot work near oil system without proper fire watch
